pi_items.source: Mathematical verification: 10 seconds * 250 Hz = 2500 samples (matches min_samples)
Mathematical verification: 20 seconds * 250 Hz = 5000 samples (matches max_samples)
OpenBCI Cyton board specification (docs.openbci.com) and conversion script (line 316: 'Sample Rate = 250 Hz'). Original PhysioNet data is 160 Hz but resampled to 250 Hz for OpenBCI compatibility.

privileged_info_summary: The source file 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt' is derived from the real PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (subject S001, run 04), which is publicly available at https://physionet.org/content/eegmmidb/1.0.0/S001/S001R04.edf. The original EDF file contains 160 Hz sampled EEG data that is resampled to 250 Hz by the installation script to match OpenBCI Cyton board specifications. All metadata claims are verified: sample_rate is 250 Hz, min/max duration of 10-20 seconds correctly corresponds to 2500-5000 samples at this sample rate. The data is real EEG recordings, not synthetic.
